title: cerinthe major subsp. oranensis
intro: Cerinthe major subsp. oranensis, also known as Honeywort, is a flowering plant species in the borage family, native to North Africa and the Iberian Peninsula. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, wounds, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Honeywort include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function, making it a popular choice in herbal remedies. The plant can be prepared as an herbal tea, infused oil, or dried herb for topical use, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
